cases.description: path1 top-heavy, path2 the outlet mirror
cases.rationale: equal end-to-end conductance (equal uncoupled outlet shares) but very different mid-node pressures -> the primary structural-discrimination case
negative control: p1==p2 at every G_lat, q_lat==0, no lateral effect
unequal end-to-end conductances -> proxy can alter shares (partial mimicry)
outlet-side mirror of top_contrast: inlet- vs outlet-side asymmetry signature
guards against conclusions being an artifact of exact mirror symmetry
scaling check: pressures/shares unchanged vs mirror; dimensional flows x10

g_axial_reference_definition: mean of the two uncoupled end-to-end series conductances g_series(g_top,g_bot)=g_top*g_bot/(g_top+g_bot)

models.proxy: frozen streamtube share homogenizer w<-(1-alpha)*w+alpha (analysis.lateral_proxy.frozen_two_path_proxy); has NO pressure law.

regime_labels_note: the 0.05/5 Lambda labels are PROVISIONAL pressure-equalization descriptors, NOT a proved proxy-discrimination theorem; see the pressure_gap_class + mathematically_distinguishable columns.
scope: FEASIBILITY discrimination only — NOT Paper-4; no experimental fit, no alpha->Lambda map, no claim espresso lies in any regime or that Lambda is measurable.
sign_convention: q_lat_1to2 = G_lat*(p1-p2) > 0 means flow path1 -> path2
